title: Colour Coding
wikitext:
[]: Colour Coding?             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Mar 06, 2007 7:02 pm This has probably been mentioned somewhere already, but I can't find any threads. Anyway, I've only recently noticed that the amber/orange colour of Gendou's glasses... ... is quite similar to the canonical colour for the AT Field in the show. Perhaps this is a visual connection. Gendou's glasses as his AT Field. A barrier he hides behind? Shielding his heart from others? Then again it could just be nothing at all. Since I'm not artistically inclined, I really have no idea if these colours can even be taken as all that similar. I also caught myself looking for orange/amber objects in scenes. This is something, or perhaps nothing, that could be taken far too far. But, they do seem to be the same colour to me. What do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by Good_Noodle » Tue Mar 06, 2007 7:38 pm Interesting thought, but I don't think Gendou has the power to form his AT field in the shape of stylish eyewear. Cool observation, nonetheless!

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Tue Mar 06, 2007 7:41 pm That's very interesting, although the colours are far from identical, the Glasses are certainly a fundamental part of his form. As well as a deviation from his, apparent, influence, that guy from whatsitcalled UFO? It puts a meaningful spin on this moment: If Rei II has his ATF... Although his first set of glasses are different he loses them when trying to rescue Rei. A moment when he lets down his defences. Edit: I'm Israfel, I wish there were two of me then I'd get more done.

[]:             Quote Post by Good_Noodle » Tue Mar 06, 2007 7:52 pm Yeah, the glasses are very symbolic of his isolation and his shunning of others. Mmmmm! I love when I dig up another juicy symbol!

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Mar 06, 2007 8:09 pm Very interesting thought. It may be very significant if indeed Rei is indeed holding his "soul" like Katayoku showed...

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Tue Mar 06, 2007 8:11 pm Interestingly the first time we see him, chronologicaly, with glasses is on the day of Yui's contact experiment. His sense of isolation is so strong that he feels he has to have an acessory to define it. Like Shinji and his SDAT. That of course implies that he knew something would happen to Yui during the CE.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Mar 06, 2007 9:35 pm Uh, wasn't it Rei III holding his soul?? :?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Mar 06, 2007 9:43 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Uh, wasn't it Rei III holding his soul?? :?

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Tue Mar 06, 2007 10:57 pm Its just a symbol showing how Rei II cared about Gendo through her life.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Mar 07, 2007 12:39 am With this new bit of information it may be more than that. In NGE, some things symbolize more than one thing. The Evas themselves are a good example of this.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Wed Mar 07, 2007 1:10 am true, but then maybe the color of each members underwear means something also?!

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Mar 07, 2007 1:19 am Fearthebait wrote: true, but then maybe the color of each members underwear means something also?!

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Wed Mar 07, 2007 1:24 am yeah, i like playing devils advocate too :P

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Mar 07, 2007 1:35 am Ornette wrote: Fearthebait wrote: true, but then maybe the color of each members underwear means something also?!

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Wed Mar 07, 2007 1:39 am *twilight zone music*

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Mar 07, 2007 4:45 am Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: If Rei II has his ATF... Although his first set of glasses are different he loses them when trying to rescue Rei. A moment when he lets down his defences.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Mar 07, 2007 2:11 pm No clue about Rei I. And perhaps the red dots hadn't come into play yet.... Arrrrgh, you're ruining my perfect theory! :x

[]:             Quote Post by C4 » Wed Mar 07, 2007 7:16 pm Hmm... AT field glasses.... that'll protect you from those pesky UV rays. But yeah, I don't think there's really anything to the connection between the two; but if we're talking about similar colors here, I think you have to mention LCL, which is the same color as both the AT field and the glasses.(Though, in some shots LCL can be light enough in color to be considered bright gold, it's usually dark enough to be the same color as the AT field.) It's interesting to note(well, as interesting as color symbology can be) that the substance that we all turn into, thus bonding us all after we die, is the same color as the physical realization of the boundaries that we use to set up barriers to avoid these bonds. But yeah, assigning color symbiology is usually grasping at straws. What it all comes down to, for me at least, is that it has to be some color. I mean, if his glasses were purple, would we be talking about his connection to Unit 01? No, the answer to that question is that we would be talking about how much of a pimp he is, and then we'd make a bunch of Redeath references. But I digress...

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Wed Mar 07, 2007 7:27 pm pretty much the glasses add to the gendo cool factor, thats it

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Mar 07, 2007 8:52 pm Agree that colors are usually grasping at straws, but think about this: Usually sunglasses are dark/black. Much of the time when we see Gendo with glasses we see light shining on them and blocking out his eyes anyway (not always, but sometimes). And often we just see his hands in front of his face and not his eyes/glasses at all. What I'm getting to is they could've just made his glasses BLACK so they wouldn't have to draw his eyes, and he could look that much more unfriendly. But making them orange is kinda strange. And the fact that orange is the same color as the ATF, which is the barrier between people, and glasses are meant to act like that, does seem to correlate rather well.
